confirm.title: Are you sure?
err.bad-mode: mode must be a 3–4 digit octal value, e.g. 0400
err.bad-owner: owner and group must look like unix account names (a-z 0-9 _ -)
flakes.empty: No flake inputs yet. Add one to pull an external flake into the system flake.
flakes.follow-hint: Pins this input's nixpkgs to the state flake's nixpkgs input, avoiding a second copy in the closure. See the README on collapsing it onto the host's nixpkgs.
flakes.intro: External flakes declared as inputs of the managed state flake. Defining an input fetches and pins it into the lock on apply; wiring an input into a workload or the host is handled separately.
flakes.name: Name
flakes.name-hint: 1–63 characters of a-z 0-9 -. The flake input name, referenced as
flakes.title: Flake inputs
flash.disabled: Disabled. Apply to remove it from the system.
flash.enabled: Enabled. Apply to make it live.
flash.secret-attached: Delivery added. Apply to deploy it.
flash.secret-detached: Delivery removed. Apply to deploy the change.
flash.secret-removed: Removed. Apply to remove it from the system.
flash.secret-saved: Saved. Apply to deploy the change.
job.title: Job #%d
locale.name: English
login.denied: This account is not allowed to administer this machine.
login.intro: Sign in with a system account that is allowed to administer this machine.
login.throttled: Too many attempts — wait a minute and try again.
login.title: Sign in
new.display-hint: A free-form label for the UI; the ID is prefilled from it.
new.draft-hint: The workload is created as a draft: it only becomes part of the system after you enable it and apply.
new.title: New workload
secrets.empty: No secrets yet. Add one to make it available to workloads.
secrets.identity-hint: Identity and permissions of the decrypted file on the host. Defaults: root, root, 0400. A container process reading the file through a mount needs matching access.
secrets.intro: Values are encrypted to this host's SSH key (age) and stored only as ciphertext; agenix decrypts them during activation. Deliver a secret into a container below, then read it at /run/agenix/<name> — the same path for NixOS and OCI containers. Host services read that path directly, no delivery needed.
secrets.mounts-hint: Containers that get the decrypted file mounted read-only at /run/agenix/<name>. Delivery to a disabled workload stays dormant until it is enabled.
secrets.name: Name
secrets.name-hint: 1–63 characters of a-z 0-9 -. Also the on-disk name and the read path:
secrets.network-hint: The first apply after creating the first secret fetches agenix (one small flake input); subsequent applies are offline as usual.
secrets.no-targets: No container workloads yet — create one to deliver secrets into it. Host services need no delivery.
secrets.remove-confirm: Remove secret %s? Workloads reading it will fail to find the file after the next apply.
secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.value-hint: Stored encrypted; never shown again. Line endings are normalized, otherwise byte-exact.
secrets.value-keep: leave blank to keep the current value
sys.dry-would-reboot: Dry-run: would reboot the machine — nothing happened.
sys.dry-would-shutdown: Dry-run: would shut down the machine — nothing happened.
sys.power-dry-hint: Dry-run mode: these buttons run through the flow but do not touch the machine.
sys.power-hint: Reboot or shut down the host running nixbox.
sys.poweroff-hint: Powers the machine off. It stays down until something turns it back on — on a headless server that means physical or out-of-band access.
sys.reboot-hint: Reboots the machine. nixbox and every container go down until it comes back up.
sys.rebooting: Rebooting… the machine will disconnect shortly.
sys.shutting-down: Shutting down… the machine will power off shortly.
term.disabled-2: to expose an interactive shell. Note this hands out a root console with no authentication — only enable it on a trusted, loopback-bound instance.
term.hint: Interactive shell on the host. The session ends when you leave this page.
wl.destroy-data-hint-1: Disables the container and rebuilds the system without it. The container's data under
wl.destroy-data-hint-2: is kept unless you check the box below.
wl.destroy-hint: Removing this workload disables it, rebuilds the system without it, then deletes it and its history. This cannot be undone.
wl.destroy-nodata-hint: Disables the workload and rebuilds the system without it. Any data it keeps elsewhere on disk (podman volumes, service state directories) is left untouched.
wl.must-run: The container must be running first.
wl.ports-hint-1: Opened on the host firewall — for ports served directly on the host's network stack (nixos-containers, host-networked OCI, or host services). Podman-published ports
wl.ports-hint-2: don't need an entry. Saved with the config.
wl.rename-hint-1: A friendly label for the UI. The workload ID
wl.rename-hint-2: is permanent and unaffected. Leave blank to just show the ID. Applies immediately — no rebuild.
wl.secrets-hint: Encrypted secrets delivered into this container, readable at the path shown. Managed system-wide on the Secrets tab.
wl.secrets-new-hint: Encrypted immediately and delivered here. Owner and permissions default to root / 0400 — adjust them on the Secrets tab. Apply to deploy.
wl.secrets-none: No secrets delivered into this workload.
